title: How to write a page in Markdown
text: This is some text just after the title, before any other section or subsection.
crumbs: How to write a page in Markdown

title: How to write a page in Markdown
text:
This is a subsection
One can use in-line LaTeX equations, like \(x^2+y^2=r^2\), or in their own line, like
\[x=\frac{-b\pm \sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{2a}\]

This is a subsubsection
This is an italic text and bold text. One can also insert an unsorted list

Element
Element
Element

And a sorted list:

Element 1
Element 2
Element 3

And a nested list:

level 1 item

level 2 item
level 2 item

level 3 item
level 3 item



This is a table:



Head 1
Head 2
Head 3




a
b
c


d
e
f



This is a quote:

“If it is on the internet… it must be true.” - Abraham Lincoln

This is a picture



Octocat


One can also insert in-line code. But one can also insert code with highlighted syntax
int suma (){
  int a, b;
  return a+b;
}
This is an internal link to a section in the same page. This is an external link to a section in another page.
Finally, one can also insert HTML code directly:
